signals.title: JARVIS operating repo
signals.detail: A private agent operating system with missions, tickets, worktrees, reports, Scribe, and Promoter.

signals.title: short memory, long repo
signals.detail: The thesis: chat is runtime exhaust; the repo is the durable product.

signals.title: Promoter
signals.detail: A publishing product that turns commits, reports, and lessons into reviewable public material.

signals.title: Proof rails
signals.detail: Nothing is done until there is a command, screenshot, report, commit hash, or live URL.

proofStories.title: The repo that survives the chat
proofStories.angle: AI work needs continuity, not longer context windows. The operating contract lives in files, not memory.
evidence: MASTERPLAN.md defines the mission sequence and acceptance demo.
MISSION_STATE.md holds current truth for every new session.
reports/sessions.log records proof lines instead of vibes.

proofStories.title: Worktrees as the unit of agent work
proofStories.angle: Main checkouts stay clean while short-lived worktrees carry isolated tasks from ticket to merge.
evidence: scripts/wt-new.ps1 creates ticket branches under C:/dev/.wt.
scripts/wt-done.ps1 merges, removes the worktree, and updates dashboard state.
Ticket dependencies are checked before a branch can start.

proofStories.title: Promoter turns proof into public material
proofStories.angle: The publishing workflow should prepare, rank, and package ideas while the human keeps editorial control.
evidence: Promoter generates LinkedIn and blog drafts from reports, commits, and lessons.
Each pack includes hooks, timing, image prompt, CTA, and checklist.
Archive controls keep posted and passed ideas out of tomorrow's queue.

proofStories.title: Scribe audits whether yesterday survived
proofStories.angle: A personal operating system needs an auditor that checks continuity, not another place to manually summarize work.
evidence: Scribe runs on a schedule and writes daily reports.
It scans local repos for fresh trace and stale assumptions.
Findings become dashboard state and backlog material.

systems.name: JARVIS
systems.role: Mission control for plans, tickets, worktrees, reports, and proof.

systems.name: Promoter
systems.role: Turns the proof trail into public writing and launch material.

systems.name: Scribe
systems.role: Audits whether daily work has durable trace and useful next actions.

systems.name: Worktree lifecycle
systems.role: Keeps agent development isolated from main checkouts.

systems.name: Router
systems.role: Sends cheap routine work to local models and keeps reasoning explicit.

writing.title: short memory.
writing.note: Why external memory is infrastructure, not a note-taking hobby.

writing.title: the context bar.
writing.note: A small UI idea for making agent work more legible.

writing.title: the package manager nobody asked for.
writing.note: Why AI capabilities need distribution and governance.

writing.title: things i didn't know.
writing.note: A longer note on learning by building systems that push back.
